thoughts.text: signal_matrix.json refreshed at 09:30 PDT (computed_at 2026-06-29T16:30Z), 2h41m old at session time — within the §2.5 3-hour window. The Monday 5:50 AM run that failed earlier has since recovered. All signal-gated actions (deposit routing, gate evaluations) are unblocked.

thoughts.label: CORE DEPLOYED 0% → 50%
thoughts.text: The ~$111 deferred idle cash routed to the most-underweight tier (CORE, was -50pp) per §17.2, split across the §13.2 three-sleeve structure: VOO $25 (S&P-500 anchor), QQQ $25 (Nasdaq-100 growth + ARKK migration target), SOXX $25 (semis + MRVL replacement §13.3). Each ~16.7% of book, well under the 25% cap. CORE now 50.1% — on its Accumulation target. $36.37 cash retained for DRIP.

thoughts.label: LAB OVER STAGING CAP — NO NEW SINGLE-STOCK ENTRIES
thoughts.text: LAB sits at 25.6% vs its 20% Accumulation cap. RXRX cleared a fresh 4/5 compound gate and CRWD a marginal 3/5, but §17.3 forbids force-selling to rebalance, so no LAB add is authorized while the sleeve is over weight — it dilutes as deposits grow the base. Capital routed to underweight CORE instead.

thoughts.label: STOPS RECALIBRATED TO ATR
thoughts.text: With a fresh matrix the LAB fractional stops moved to ATR-based: INTC 5%→12.6% (ATR 8.38%, BULLISH 5/5 — a 5% stop sat inside one ATR and would whipsaw, the MRVL lesson); ARKK 7%→5.2% (tighter/more protective); NVDA kept 5% (composite BEARISH -0.502, Trend50=Down — high-failure-risk, do not loosen).

thoughts.label: DRIP BLOCKED + EMAIL DOWN
thoughts.text: DRIP is now the most-underweight tier (-30pp) but entries are blocked: the dividend engine still emits broken data (yields >100%, null ex-dates, streak 0), so the §15.3 screen can't be verified. Separately, RESEND_API_KEY is absent in this environment — the Daily Brief email could not send; fell back to PushNotification (§10.3).

learned_patterns.pattern: Deferred idle cash is deployed via deposit-routing to the single most-underweight tier (§17.2), not a signal-based single-stock entry. CORE was -50pp underweight, so the ~$111 cash routed to CORE across the §13.2 three-sleeve structure (anchor/growth/semis), taking it 0%→50%. DRIP (-30pp) is next.

An over-weight tier blocks new entries into it even on a strong signal: LAB at 25.6% > 20% cap means RXRX's fresh 4/5 gate does not authorize an add, because §17.3 forbids force-selling to rebalance. Over-weight sleeves dilute via base growth, not trimming.

Flat 5% stops whipsaw out of high-ATR names (the MRVL -5.71% stop-out). With a fresh matrix, stops moved to clamp(1.5x ATR%): INTC's 8.38% ATR justifies a 12.6% stop, while a BEARISH/downtrend NVDA stays tight at 5% (high-failure-risk caps downside).

The Signal Engine missed the Monday 5:50 AM run (60h-stale matrix forced SAFE MODE) but recovered by 09:30 PDT. The §2.5 guard blocked trading on stale data, then released cleanly once fresh — idle cash deployed the same day.
MU's +11.78% post-earnings spike fully reversed (-7.46%), validating the Z>2.0 entry block. PANW now sits Z=2.82 OVERBOUGHT — same block applies.

The dividend engine emits broken values (yields >100%, null ex-dates, streak 0), blocking DRIP entries and forcing an empty dividend_matrix (§11.9). Tier build is gated on data quality, not just allocation math.
